reasoning: Bobby Finke set the 1500m long course WR in Paris 2024 (14:30.67), breaking a legendary 12-year-old record by Sun Yang. Historically, the 1500m freestyle WR has incredible longevity: Salnikov, Perkins, Hackett, and Sun Yang all held the record for around a decade each. The primary threats are Daniel Wiffen and Ahmed Hafnaoui, but bridging the gap to 14:30.67 is profoundly difficult. Additionally, 2025 is a post-Olympic year where times are typically slightly slower. Given Finke could also break his own record, the probability of another swimmer taking the WR before the close date is extremely low.
